label: Selective state-space scan

node.label: Selective state-space scan
node.definition: Input-dependent state-space parameters selectively propagate or forget information while preserving linear-time scans.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Selective state-space scan is modeled as depending on State-space transitions, Gating & multiplicative control and feeding downstream nodes such as Selective state-space models (Mamba family).
examples: atlas downstream link to Selective state-space models (Mamba family)
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include State-space transitions, Gating & multiplicative control.

dependencies.rationale: Selective state-space scan is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component State-space transitions.
Selective state-space scan is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
In this atlas graph, Selective state-space scan has a `uses` dependency on Algorithms & data structures because Algorithms & data structures is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
